message:
fix(block): don't restore dirty state when repair notification fails after successful flush

Previously, when flush_blocks() successfully wrote blocks to device but
notify_repair_flush() subsequently failed, the code would call
restore_pending_flush_candidates() to re-mark the blocks as dirty and
return an error. This was incorrect: the blocks are already durably
persisted on the device — only the repair subsystem missed the
notification.

Re-marking persisted blocks as dirty caused:
- Unnecessary re-flushes of already-written data
- Potential write amplification in degraded repair scenarios
- False "flush failed" errors propagated to callers despite data safety

Fix applied to three flush paths:
1. pending_flush_batch (periodic flush of pending candidates)
2. flush_batch (explicit batch flush)
3. flush_dirty (background dirty block flush)

In all three cases, the repair notification failure is now logged as a
warning but the flush is considered successful. The dirty bits are
properly cleared via guard.clear_dirty() in the pending_flush_batch
path, and the existing guard.dirty_flushes counter is correctly
incremented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
